title: LLM Evaluation — Benchmarks, Automated Evals & CI/CD Regression
description: At Staff level, harness engineering is about building robust evaluation pipelines that measure model quality, detect regressions, and guide data/architecture decisions. Covers benchmark selection, automated evals, human eval at scale, and continuous monitoring.

sections.title: LLM Benchmarks Taxonomy & Contamination Mitigation
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Benchmarks measure specific capabilities. Taxonomy: Knowledge (MMLU, GPQA), Reasoning (GSM8K, MATH), Code (HumanEval, SWE-Bench), Safety (TruthfulQA). **Data Contamination** is a major issue: test data leaks into training sets. To prevent this, Staff Engineers build clean filters (e.g. n-gram sliding hashes of test sets) to scan training crawls before model runs start.

bar_chart Contamination check: A 13-gram exact match between a pre-training document and GSM8K test questions indicates contamination, requiring immediate document removal.

track_changes Heuristic Strategy: Filter documents containing exact matches of known question fragments, retaining statistics to verify test-set sanitization.

sections.title: Building a Distributed Evaluation Harness
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Evaluating large models over multiple benchmarks is computationally expensive. A distributed evaluation harness schedules independent (model, task) pairs across a cluster of GPUs. Results are cached globally in key-value stores to avoid repeating identical tasks.

bar_chart Throughput boost: Dynamic batching and parallel task distribution reduce total evaluation time for a 70B model from 3+ hours to ~15 minutes across a 4-GPU cluster.

sections.title: Automated Evals with LLM-as-Judge & Calibration
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Prompting an LLM (e.g. GPT-4) to evaluate another model's output introduces position bias, verbosity bias, and self-similarity bias. **Calibration** solves this by randomizing response ordering, using strict rubrics with anchor examples, and calculating agreement rates with human ground truth labels.

bar_chart Position bias: A judge LLM prefers response A over B by ~65% when content is equal. Mitigate by running evaluations twice with order swapped (A/B and B/A) and taking the mean.
sections.codeBlock:
def judge_pairwise_calibrated(prompt, resp_a, resp_b, judge_func):
    # Eval 1: A first
    pref_1 = judge_func(prompt, resp_a, resp_b) # returns A or B
    # Eval 2: B first (swapped)
    pref_2 = judge_func(prompt, resp_b, resp_a) # returns A or B
    
    # Return final preference, resolving disagreements as ties
    if pref_1 == 'A' and pref_2 == 'B': # B was preferred in second
         return 'tie'
    return pref_1 if pref_1 == pref_2 else 'tie'

sections.title: Human Evaluation at Scale: Cohen's Kappa & Sample Sizes
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Human evaluation is the final verification stage. To scale human review, we must calculate the required sample size using power analysis and verify labeler quality using **Cohen's Kappa ($\kappa$)** to measure inter-annotator agreement above random chance.

bar_chart Cohen's Kappa: Target $\kappa > 0.6$ (substantial agreement). Reject labelers with low agreement on golden control tasks.
sections.codeBlock:
import numpy as np

def calculate_cohens_kappa(labels_a, labels_b):
    agree = np.mean(labels_a == labels_b)
    # Compute chance probability
    p_a = np.bincount(labels_a) / len(labels_a)
    p_b = np.bincount(labels_b) / len(labels_b)
    p_chance = np.sum(p_a * p_b)
    return (agree - p_chance) / (1 - p_chance)

sections.title: Regression Testing in CI/CD pipelines
sections.description:
psychology Intuition: Code PRs can trigger silent performance degradation in downstream models. A fast **Golden Test Set** (200-500 diverse examples) must run on every commit in the CI/CD pipeline, gating merges if score drops exceed a defined threshold.

bar_chart Gating rule: If golden set accuracy drops > 2%, or safety scores regress, block the pull request immediately.

sections.title: References & Further Reading
sections.description:
• LM Eval Harness — EleutherAI's standard evaluation framework.
  • Judging LLM-as-a-Judge — Zheng et al., 2023.

details: Staff Engineer Summary: Harness engineering is the core of iteration speed. Implement auto-evals (LLM-as-judge) with positional calibration, sanitize data to prevent contamination, and run lightweight golden sets on every commit inside CI/CD gates to protect model stability.
